sentences.english: I think I'm not very good at keeping clean.
My wife is always getting upset that I'm leaving things out on the counter in the kitchen, or leaving my clothes in the bedroom in the wrong place.
sentences.korean: 부엌 조리대에 물건을 놓아두거나 침실에 옷을 엉뚱한 곳에 두면 아내가 항상 화내요.

sentences.english: I procrastinate. This means to kind of put stuff off.
sentences.korean: 미루는 버릇이 있어요. 중요한 일을 마지막 순간까지 남겨두죠.

sentences.english: I often leave really important things until the last minute.
sentences.korean: 할일 목록으로 극복하려 하지만 여전히 떨쳐낼 수 없어요.

sentences.english: I try to fight it by making to-do lists, but it's still a part of me that I really can't shake off.
sentences.korean: / 할일 목록으로 극복하려 하지만, 떨쳐낼 수 없는 나의 일부다.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: When I see somebody, sometimes I tend to judge the person too quickly.
sentences.korean: 누군가를 볼 때 너무 빨리 판단하는 경향이 있어요.

sentences.english: And I think that's a very bad habit.
My biggest bad habit is wasting money.
sentences.korean: 가장 큰 나쁜 습관은 돈 낭비예요.

sentences.english: I often eat out just for the sake of eating out.
sentences.korean: 그냥 외식하려고 외식하고, 그냥 사려고 물건을 사고 아마 절대 안 쓸 거예요.

sentences.english: I often buy something just for the sake of buying it, and I will probably never use it.
sentences.korean: / 그냥 사려고 물건을 사고 아마 절대 안 쓸 것이다.

sentences.english: I have a bad habit of sleeping too much.
sentences.korean: 너무 많이 자는 나쁜 습관이 있어요.

sentences.english: If I'm even the slightest bit tired, I'll usually lie down and take a nap, but that makes it very difficult for me to wake up in the morning.
sentences.korean: 조금이라도 피곤하면 보통 누워서 낮잠을 자는데 아침에 일어나기가 매우 어려워요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
